# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

_format_version: "2.1"
plugins:
- name: cors
  enabled: true
  config:
    origins: "http://example.com, https://example.org"
    methods: "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS"
    headers: "Content-Type, Authorization"
    max_age: 3600
    credentials: true

- name: key-auth
  enabled: true

- name: rate-limiting
  enabled: true
  config:
    second: 5
    hour: 1000
    limit_by: consumer

services:
- name: example-service
  url: http://example-backend.com
  plugins:
  - name: key-auth
    enabled: true

  - name: rate-limiting
    enabled: true
    config:
      second: 3
      hour: 500

routes:
- name: example-route
  paths: ["/api/v1"]
  service: example-service
  plugins:
  - name: cors
    enabled: true
    config:
      origins: "http://example.com, https://example.org"
      methods: "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS"
      headers: "Content-Type, Authorization"
      max_age: 3600
      credentials: true

- name: another-route
  paths: ["/api/v2"]
  service: example-service
  plugins:
  - name: key-auth
    enabled: true
